verses:
citkaṇa jīva, kṛṣṇa cinmaya bhāskara
nitya kṛṣṇa dekhi’ kṛṣṇe karena ādara

kṛṣṇa bahir⋅mukha hoo bhoga⋅vāñchā kare
nikaṭastha māyā tāre jāpaṭiyā dhare

piśācī pāile jena mati⋅cchanna haya
māyā⋅grasta jīvera haya se⋅bhāva udaya

‘āmi siddha kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa’⋅ei kathā bhule’
māyāra naphara hoo cira⋅dina bule

kabhu rājā, kabhu prajā, kabhu vipra, śūdra
kabhu duḥkhī, kabhu sukhī, kabhu kīṭa kṣudra

kabhu svarge, kabhu martye, narake vā kabhu
kabhu deva, kabhu daitya, kabhu dāsa prabhu

ei⋅rūpe saṁsāra bhramite kona⋅jana
sādhu⋅saṅge nija⋅tattva avagata hana

nija⋅tattva jāni’ āra saṁsāra nā cāya
‘kena vā bhajinu māyā’⋅kare hāya hāya

ke̐de bale,⋅“ohe kṛṣṇa, āmi tava dāsa
tomāra caraṇa chāḍi’ haila sarvanāśa”

kākuti kariyā kṛṣṇe jadi ḍāke eka⋅bāra
kṛpā kari’ kṛṣṇa tā’re chāḍāna saṁsāra

māyāke pichane rākhi’ kṛṣṇa⋅pāne cāya
bhajite bhajite kṛṣṇa⋅pāda⋅padma pāya

kṛṣṇa tā’re dena nija cicchaktira bala
māyā ākarṣaṇa chāḍe hoiyā durbala

‘sādhu⋅saṅge kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’⋅ei⋅mātra cāi
saṁsāra jinite āra kona vastu nāi
en_translation:
The jīva is a particle of consciousness; Kṛṣṇa is the sun of consciousness. Seeing Kṛṣṇa eternally, he shows affection to Kṛṣṇa.

Turning away from Kṛṣṇa, desiring enjoyment, the Māyā nearby catches him and holds him tight.

Just as one's intelligence becomes covered when possessed by a ghost, that state arises in the jīva gripped by Māyā.

Forgetting the fact "I am an eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa," he becomes a slave of Māyā and wanders forever.

Sometimes a king, sometimes a subject, sometimes a brāhmaṇa, a śūdra. Sometimes unhappy, sometimes happy, sometimes a small insect.

Sometimes in heaven, sometimes in the mortal world, or sometimes in hell. Sometimes a god, sometimes a demon, sometimes a servant, a master.

Wandering in the material world in this way, some person becomes aware of his own truth in the association of a saint.

Knowing his own truth, he no longer wants the material world. He laments, "Alas, alas! Why did I worship Māyā?"

Weeping, he says: "O Kṛṣṇa, I am Your servant. Leaving Your feet, I have been ruined."

If he calls Kṛṣṇa once with piteous words, Kṛṣṇa mercifully releases him from the material world.

Keeping Māyā behind, he looks towards Kṛṣṇa. Worshipping and worshipping, he attains Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet.

Kṛṣṇa gives him the strength of His own spiritual energy. Māyā, becoming weak, lets go of her attraction.

'Kṛṣṇa-nāma in the association of saints'—this is all I want. There is no other object to conquer the material world.
